content:
The "Trust junk mail headers set by" option is found in Account Settings, not the program-wide Settings. There's a separate setting for each account. This setting is to be used if your incoming email has been rated by an external filtering service.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-and-junk-spam-messages#w_use-an-external-filter-service
creator: rednoise

content: That option only suppresses the dialog shown when you click Send. If you want to delay sending until you send it from the Outbox manually, press Ctrl-Shift-Enter. If you want to send it automatically at a later date, get the Send Later add-on - https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/send-later-3/.
creator: siffemoz

content:
"Thunderbird Pro" will be a suite of subscription services that includes an email host (ThunderMail) a Calendar/Scheduling server (Appointment) and a file-sharing service (Send). As I understand it, Thunderbird will use an add-on to integrate it closely with the Pro services, but the services can also be used by many other email clients. It will not replace Thunderbird. It's not available yet and I haven't heard a release date yet, but I get the sense that it's still some ways off.

A description:

https://blog.thunderbird.net/2025/04/thundermail-and-thunderbird-pro-services/

The main site where you can sign up for a waiting list:

https://www.tb.pro/en-US/
creator: rednoise

content:
Thunderbird hasn't had any major interface changes for the past year or so, so I have a feeling any visual changes you find are due to being setup differently. The Linux and Windows version have always looked almost identical. That is to say, that you may not get what you're looking for by downgrading. But if you are determined to do so, here's a guide. Be sure to back up your current profile before proceeding.

To downgrade Thunderbird:

1. Download your preferred older version of Thunderbird from

<https://releases.mozilla.org/pub/thunderbird/releases/>.

Be mindful to get the proper 32- or 64- bit version for your computer platform.

2. Delete the COMPATIBILITY.INI file from Thunderbird's profile folder: 

  a. Open Thunderbird   b. Open the Troubleshooting page via Menu <Help | Troubleshooting information>   c. Click Profile Folder -> Open folder   d. Close Thunderbird   e. At this point you should make a backup of everything in this folder just in case anything goes wrong   f. Delete COMPATIBILITY.INI from the folder

3. Use the installer to install the older version, overwriting the current version.

4. Launch the now-downgraded Thunderbird.
creator: rednoise

content:
Hello,

Delete the old credentials:

Go to Menu > Settings > Privacy & Security.

Under the Passwords section, click Saved Passwords....

Find your Gmail account

Select and Remove.

Restart Thunderbird.

Once you restart after deleting the password, Thunderbird will trigger a Google login popup where you can enter your new password.

More info Password Manager - Remember, delete and change passwords in Thunderbird

content:
You can do this with the following CSS code 

 #threadTree  tbody [data-properties~="tagged"] {   background-color: var(--tag-color) !important;   color: var(--tag-contrast-color) !important; }


My first attached image shows that this code works for me. But I don't use it since with this code I would no longer distinguish between selected tagged messages and unselected tagged messages, in focus and not in focus, they appear all the same having the same tag. My second image shows the default thread pane without the CSS code

content:
You can certainly do that, but you have set it up. The solution is to create a template for sending and for replying. More on templates is at https://kb.mozillazine.org/Message_templates With that knowledge, you will have templates that you can manually click to when sending or replying. To have that done automatically each time you send or reply, you could use the smarttemplates addon, located at this URL: https://services.addons.thunderbird.net/en-us/thunderbird/addon/smarttemplate4/

You may need to experiment with it a bit, sending and replying to yourself. Have fun. :)
creator: davidsk
